mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically reducing present equity values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window, or mean return in the highest FEDFUNDS quartile is not significantly lower than the lowest quartile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate that rises more than 0.5 percentage points above its 12-month trailing minimum signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate income and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the return distribution conditional on the trigger is not significantly left-shifted relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated while retail sales are simultaneously contracting, the joint pressure from tight credit and weak consumer spending produces sharply negative S&P 500 returns that neither factor alone predicts.
failure_condition: A two-factor interaction model does not significantly outperform a univariate benchmark using either FEDFUNDS or RSAFS alone in predicting 20-day SPX returns (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals supply-chain stress and falling corporate output, predicting negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly more negative during FEDFUNDS hiking cycles than during easing cycles (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in new housing starts reflects expanding construction activity and forward-looking household confidence, which raises cyclical corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between HOUST and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation above 4% annualized forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating real discount rates and producing negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Falling nonfarm payrolls coinciding with declining retail sales create a reinforcing contraction in both household income and consumer expenditure, generating sharply negative S&P 500 returns that exceed either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS and RSAFS does not add explanatory power beyond main effects in a 20-day forward return regression (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1% annualized signals near-stagnant aggregate demand, which compresses top-line revenue growth across the index and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 45% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is below 1%.
